{{Quote|The only new people that come to Junix Nard's cantina are longtime losers and bounty hunters. I don't have to tell you why the two don't mix well. Please keep any blaster fire to a minimum.|Junix to [[Revan/Legends|Revan]]|<ref name="KotOR" />}}
'''Junix's Joint''', also known as the '''Anchorhead Cantina''', was a thousand-years old [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]] in [[Anchorhead/Legends|Anchorhead]] on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] after the end of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]].

==History==
The Anchorhead Cantina was the social and dining establishment of the city of [[Anchorhead/Legends|Anchorhead]] on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] during the [[Jedi Civil War]]. Around [[3956 BBY]] the [[Cantina/Legends|cantina]] was operated by [[Junix Nard]], who was also the [[Bartender/Legends|bartender]]. He employed two [[Jawa/Legends|Jawa]] servers, and the establishment featured two [[Bith/Legends|Bith]] musicians and a [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] dancer, as well as a Bith [[Hologram/Legends|hologram]]. Nard also ran a small store, but had limited selection as [[Czerka Arms/Legends|Czerka]] only worried about keeping their own stores full.<ref name="KotOR" />

[[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] [[Revan/Legends|Revan]] and [[Bastila Shan]] stopped by the cantina in 3956 BBY during their search for the Tatooine [[Star Map]]. There Bastila encountered her mother, [[Helena Shan|Helena]], whom she had not seen in many years. Also in the cantina at the time was a [[Swoop racing/Legends|swoop racing]] veteran named [[Gandroff]], a [[Pazaak/Legends|pazaak]] player named [[Furko Nellis]], and two Czerka officers, as well as a handful of other patrons.<ref name="KotOR" />

It seemed that the bar was a place favored by Czerka officers to relax and worry about their job issues when not working at the Czerka office. Several local people were worrying about issues with the war at the time Revan visited, fearing that if [[Darth Malak]] would demolish [[Taris/Legends|Taris]]-a valuable world with billions of people- to defeat the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Republic]], what grim fate would come to Tatooine if he arrived. However, their fears were unfounded as the [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] was infinitely more concerned with capturing Bastila Shan and conquering the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]] than seizing a remote planet with nothing to offer but sand, [[Tusken Raider/Legends|savage natives]] and worthless ore.

In the early [[Standard day/Legends|days]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], the cantina, by this point known as Junix's Joint, was staffed by a [[Duros/Legends|Duros]] [[Junix's Joint bartender|bartender]] and his [[Marriage/Legends|wife]], [[Yoona]]. [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] stopped at the cantina with the infant [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] to ask for directions to the [[Lars homestead/Legends|Lars homestead]] in [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]], in the process becoming involved in a [[Skirmish at Junix's Joint|skirmish]] involving several of [[Jabba Desilijic Tiure/Legends|Jabba]] the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutts]] thugs.<ref name="Kenobi">[[Kenobi (novel)|''Kenobi'']]</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
Junix's Joint first appeared in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Knights of the Old Republic]]'' under the ownership of Junix Nard.<ref name="KotOR" /> In [[John Jackson Miller]]'s [[2013]] novel [[Kenobi (novel)|''Kenobi'']], Obi-Wan Kenobi visits an Anchorhead cantina called Junix's Joint.<ref name="Kenobi" /> Miller confirmed on his [[Wikipedia:Twitter|Twitter]] page that the cantina appearing in ''Kenobi'' is the same one from ''Knights of the Old Republic'', albeit under different ownership.<ref name="JJM">{{Twitter|jjmfaraway|status/369864881916239872|[[John Jackson Miller]]|quote=KENOBI visits a cantina seen in the first KOTOR game. Under new ownership, naturally!|archivefile=File:JunixsJoint-Twitter.png}}</ref>
